body.title: CC session name regression: launches as hostname-YYMMDD.HHMMSS not hostname.cc.0
body.result: devstral fixed superclaude + constants.py; CC fixed 2 test stubs that leaked real cc_session.txt — 9 tests green
body.intention: I intend that CC always launches with a stable hostname.cc.0 tmux session name.
body.description:
On 2026-06-11 CC launched as 'akiendelllinux-260611.095720' instead of 'akiendelllinux.cc.0'. T-cc-session-hostname-naming was closed but the fix did not make it into the running binary/launch path.

Need to find where the session name is set (bin/superclaude or equivalent launcher) and verify hostname.cc.0 format is enforced. T-superclaude-session-name-sync was also closed — check if the bin/superclaude script is actually what gets invoked on this machine.

**Affected files:** bin/superclaude (or equivalent CC launcher), devices/claude/constants.py (_detect_session_name)
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** In: trace launch path, fix session name to hostname.cc.0. Out: multi-instance .cc.1 support.
**Completion criteria:** Start a new CC session; tmux shows it as akiendelllinux.cc.0 (or equivalent hostname.cc.0).
